titleStmt.title: Letter from Marie Martin to her mother Mary Lewis Martin, 23 June 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Marie Martin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Marie Martin (1892-1975) to her mother Mary Lewis Martin (née Moore). Marie thanks her mother for her reply and comments on her daily activities along with her roommate. She expects to be busy soon as the hospital is evacuated. Marie mentions her brother Charlie (1895-1915), missing since December on the Salonika front, and hopes to hear from him soon.Marie was later the founder of the Medical Missionaries of Mary. At the outbreak of war in 1914 Marie joined the Voluntary Aid Detachment, a division of the Red Cross. She returned home briefly in 1916 following the death of her brother in battle until be called to serve again two months later in a field hospital in Heraldo France.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
15/June/1916 No.25 General Hospital My very dearest Motherâ A thousand thanks for your
letter dated 21st June which I thank you
for already on a Post Card it was just
lovely hearing from you, as it seemed
weeks before we got any, imagine we are
only one week & day here toâday & we
all feel like as if we have been months
from Home. However we are expecting to
be very busy very soon as the whole
Hospital is being evacuate, so our
work for the last few days
has been Spring Cleaning with a
great deal of scrubbing & real hard
work. I get on with my sister alright
but she told me straight the other
day, she does not care for V.A.Ds
& that they were more a nuisance
than anything else, so I bowed!
This was said to me after I
had been in the Division for a few
hours. However I do my best & all seems
to be going fairly well. I had a
half day toâday so went in by tram
to Boulogne & just had time for
tea & a short walk, Sunday is not
a very good day to go to town but
it is my 1st 1/2 day off. The hours
are much to same as Malta
except we never get a whole dayâ
Many thanks for forwarding
Mrs Garthans nice long letter, she
says Gerald has been recalled as
Commanding Officer to the trenches
so is naturally very anxious
about him, I hope by this you
will have had some news of Tommy
that is the worst of the East the
mails are so irregularâ I hope the
War Office will be able to find out
some satisfactory news of poor Charlie
I am afraid it looks very badly
now of him turning up but I do
hope that we shall hear something about
him poor boy â all we can do is
prayâ & hope he did not suffer much.
I has been quite easy to get to Mass
here so far, & the Chaplain seems very
niceâ There are very R.C. here just at
present. I have just killed a very
large mosquito there are a good few
Knocking about hereâ How did Ethel get on at the Inspecâ
tion & in the team, did they carry
off the cupâ I hope the pound
day at Temple Hill was a great
success & made plenty of money How is Uncle Frank, I think of him
so often & all his sufferings. I am
very glad indeed to hear Aunt
May is going away for a change
I hope you have not forgotten
about your little changeâ somewhereâ
My toe is much better, I put a
little padding in under the toe nail
which has relieved it. Could you
send me out a couple of ofsets of
White Collars & Cuffs for Blue Dress, one
1/11 Black Patent Leather Belt width 2Â½ ins
at Roberts for Paul. I am frightfully sorry to hear about
the cook, she was a rotter & all the trouble
she has given you. Now I must close as it is getting
very late & lights must go out =
Have the photos arrived yet, I
wonder what they are like! -
With the fondest love & a big
kiss to you & all at home - your ever loving & grateful daughter. Marie
